source_title: Amendment in export policy of sugar
question: What is the revised export policy for Sugar under Chapter 17 of ITC (HS) as per Notification No. 16/2026-27?
answer: As per Notification No. 16/2026-27, the export policy of Sugar under Chapter 17 of ITC (HS) is amended from 'Restricted' to 'Prohibited' with immediate effect till September 30, 2026, or until further orders, whichever is earlier, for ITC (HS) Codes 1701 14 90 and 1701 99 90.

source_title: Amendment in export policy of sugar
question: Are there any exceptions to the prohibition on sugar exports as per Notification No. 16/2026-27?
answer: Yes, the prohibition on sugar exports does not apply to Sugar being exported to the EU and USA under CXL and TRQ quota, as well as exports under the Advance Authorization Scheme (AAS), and Government-to-Government exports, as per Notification No. 16/2026-27.

source_title: Amendment in export policy of sugar
question: What are the conditions under which export of Sugar shall be permitted despite the prohibition as per Notification No. 16/2026-27?
answer: Export of Sugar shall be permitted if the loading of sugar on the ship has commenced before the date of publication of Notification No. 16/2026-27, or if the Shipping Bill has been filed and the vessel has berthed or arrived and anchored in an Indian port before the date of publication, or if the sugar consignment has been handed over to Customs/Custodian before the date of publication and is registered in their electronic system.

answer: The purpose of Notification No. 11/2026-27 dated 17 April 2026 is to amend Notification No. 65/2025-26 to include Egypt and Jordan under the Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation (RELIEF) scheme.

source_title: Amendment to Paragraph 4.43 of the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023 -One-time relaxation for the Gems and Jewellery Sector.
question: What is the purpose of Notification No. 13/2020-26 dated March 31, 2026, issued by the Ministry of Commerce & Industry?
answer: The purpose of Notification No. 13/2020-26 is to amend Paragraph 4.43 of the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023, providing a one-time relaxation for the Gems and Jewellery sector, specifically for exporters of cut and polished diamonds, to mitigate hardship due to the ongoing geo-political crisis in West Asia.

source_title: Amendment to Paragraph 4.43 of the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023 -One-time relaxation for the Gems and Jewellery Sector.
question: What is the effect of the amendment to Paragraph 4.43 of the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023, as per Notification No. 13/2020-26?
answer: The effect of the amendment is that specified category of exporters of cut and polished diamonds will now have a total of four months (instead of three months) to avail zero-duty re-import facility in Para 4.43 of FTP for shipments whose date of re-import falls within March 01, 2026, and May 31, 2026, due to the automatic extension of the re-import period by 30 days.

question: What is the condition for availing the extended re-import period, as per Notification No. 13/2020-26?
answer: The condition for availing the extended re-import period is that the re-import must fall within the period of March 01, 2026, and May 31, 2026, and the shipment must be of cut and polished diamonds, as specified in Paragraph 4.43 of the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2023.

source_title: Continuation of RoDTEP Scheme beyond March 31, 2026
question: What is the duration for which the RoDTEP Scheme has been extended beyond March 31, 2026?
answer: As per Notification No. 74/2025-26 dated March 31, 2026, the RoDTEP Scheme shall continue for all eligible export products for a further period of six months with effect from April 01, 2026, to September 30, 2026.

source_title: Continuation of RoDTEP Scheme beyond March 31, 2026
question: Will there be any changes in the rates and value caps of the RoDTEP Scheme during the extended period?
answer: No, as notified in the amendment, the rates and value caps as notified in Appendix 4R and Appendix 4RE, as applicable on March 31, 2026, shall continue unchanged during the extended period from April 01, 2026, to September 30, 2026.

source_title: Continuation of RoDTEP Scheme beyond March 31, 2026
question: What are the terms and conditions for eligible exports to be entitled to RoDTEP benefit during the extended period?
answer: Eligible exports made during the period from 01.04.2026 to 30.09.2026 shall continue to be entitled to RoDTEP benefit at the rates and value caps in force as on 01.03.2026, subject to the existing terms and conditions of the Scheme as per Notification No. 74/2025-26.

answer: The Central Government has amended the Import Policy Conditions for Tur/Pigeon Peas under the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP), 2023, as amended from time to time, and in exercise of powers conferred by Section 3 read with Section 5 of the Foreign Trade (Development and Regulation) Act, 1992 (22 of 1992), read with paragraphs 1.02 and 2.01 of the FTP, 2023.

question: How will the RoDTEP rates be amended as per the Notification No. 60/2025-26?
answer: The applicable RoDTEP rates for all HS Lines, as notified in Appendix 4R and Appendix 4RE, shall be limited to 50% of the existing rates and, where applicable, 50% of the notified value caps, with immediate effect.

answer: According to Notification No. 53/2025-26, the import of Low Ash Metallurgical Coke with phosphorous content up to 0.030% and size up to 30 mm with 5% size tolerance for use in ferroalloy manufacturing is 'Restricted' and shall be permitted subject to Policy Condition No. 08 of Chapter 27.

source_title: Amendment in Import Policy Condition of Sulfadiazine API covered under Chapter 29 of ITC (HS), 2022, Schedule-I (Import Policy)-regarding.
question: What is the revised import policy condition for Sulfadiazine API covered under Chapter 29 of ITC (HS), 2022, as per Notification No. 41/2025-26?
answer: As per Notification No. 41/2025-26, the import of Sulfadiazine API having a CIF value of less than Rs. 1,774 per kg is 'Restricted' till 30.09.2026. However, this restriction does not apply to imports by Advance Authorization holders, Export Oriented Units (EOUs), and units in the SEZs, subject to the condition that the imported inputs are not sold into the Domestic Tariff Area (DTA).

answer: Yes, as per Notification No. 41/2025-26, Advance Authorization holders, Export Oriented Units (EOUs), and units in the SEZs are exempt from the MIP condition for Sulfadiazine API imports, provided that the imported inputs are not sold into the Domestic Tariff Area (DTA).

source_title: Amendment in Export Policy Condition under HSN 1006 of Schedule-II (Export Policy), ITC(HS) 2022-reg.
question: Which countries require a Certificate of Inspection by Export Inspection Council/Export Inspection Agency for the export of rice (Basmati and Non-Basmati) under the amended policy?
answer: As per Notification No. 39/2025-26, a Certificate of Inspection by Export Inspection Council/Export Inspection Agency is required for export of rice (Basmati and Non-Basmati) to EU Member States and European countries namely United Kingdom, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland.

source_title: RODTEP implementation for exports of products manufactured by AA holders (except Deemed Exports) and EOU for 166 Tariff lines - reg.
question: What is the purpose of DGFT Notification No. 74/2023 dated 11/03/2024?
answer: The purpose of DGFT Notification No. 74/2023 dated 11/03/2024 is to implement the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (RODTEP) scheme for exports of products manufactured by Advance Authorization (AA) holders (except Deemed Exports) and Export Oriented Units (EOU) for 166 Tariff lines.

source_title: RODTEP implementation for exports of products manufactured by AA holders (except Deemed Exports) and EOU for 166 Tariff lines - reg.
question: How many Tariff lines are covered under the RODTEP scheme as per DGFT Notification No. 74/2023?
answer: As per DGFT Notification No. 74/2023, a total of 166 Tariff lines are covered under the RODTEP scheme for exports of products manufactured by AA holders (except Deemed Exports) and EOU.

source_title: RODTEP implementation for exports of products manufactured by AA holders (except Deemed Exports) and EOU for 166 Tariff lines - reg.
question: What is the format of the annexure provided in DGFT Notification No. 74/2023?
answer: The annexure provided in DGFT Notification No. 74/2023 contains three columns: S. No., RoDTEP Entry No., and Tariff Item, listing the 166 Tariff lines covered under the RODTEP scheme, with corresponding RoDTEP Entry Nos. and Tariff Items such as 7007, 71011010, etc.

question: What are the permitted food commodities for export through National Cooperative Exports Limited (NCEL) as per Notification No. 46/2023 dated 30/11/2023?
answer: As per Notification No. 46/2023 dated 30/11/2023, the permitted food commodities for export through National Cooperative Exports Limited (NCEL) include Wheat Grain, Wheat Flour (Atta), Maida/Semolina, and Broken Rice, with specific quantities allocated for export to countries such as Bhutan, Mali, Senegal, Gambia, and Indonesia.

source_title: Harmonisation of Schedule-Il (Export Policy), ITC (US) 2022 with amendments introduced vide Finance Act, 2025 -regarding
question: Is a Certificate of Inspection by Export Inspection Council/Export Inspection Agency mandatory for export to all European countries?
answer: No, as per the notification, the Certificate of Inspection by Export Inspection Council/Export Inspection Agency shall not be mandatory for export to remaining European countries with effect from the date of the Notification for a period of 6 months, except for export to EU Member States and European countries, namely United Kingdom, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland.

source_title: Notification of ‘Indian Trade Classification (Harmonised System) of Export Items, 2023’ [Chapter 01-39 of Schedule 2, Export Policy of ITC (HS), 2023].
question: What is the export policy for live exotic birds as per the ITC (HS), 2023?
answer: As per the ITC (HS), 2023, live exotic birds are not permitted to be exported except for certain species such as Albino budgerigars, Budgerigars, Bengali finches, White finches, Zebra finches, and Jawa sparrow, which are either free subject to pre-shipment inspection or restricted and require a license and certificate from the Chief Wildlife warden.

source_title: Notification of ‘Indian Trade Classification (Harmonised System) of Export Items, 2023’ [Chapter 01-39 of Schedule 2, Export Policy of ITC (HS), 2023].
question: What are the policy conditions for the export of horses as per Notification No. 60/2023 dated 13/02/2024?
answer: As per Notification No. 60/2023 dated 13/02/2024, all horses are free except for Kathiawari, Marwari, and Manipuri breeds, which are permitted for export under a license. Additionally, pure-bred breeding animals and horses for Polo are restricted and subject to policy condition 2 of the chapter.

source_title: Notification of ‘Indian Trade Classification (Harmonised System) of Export Items, 2023’ [Chapter 01-39 of Schedule 2, Export Policy of ITC (HS), 2023].
question: What is the export policy for live bovine animals, specifically cattle, as per the ITC (HS), 2023?
answer: As per the ITC (HS), 2023, the export of live bovine animals, specifically cattle, is restricted. Pure-bred breeding animals, including bulls and cows, are restricted and exports are permitted under a license. Other cattle, including bulls and calves, are also restricted and require a license for export.

source_title: Imposition of Minimum Export Price on export of Onions
question: What is the Minimum Export Price imposed on the export of onions as per Notification No. 42/2023?
answer: The Minimum Export Price (MEP) imposed on the export of onions is US $ 800 F.O.B per Metric Ton (MT) till 31st December, 2023, as per Notification No. 42/2023 dated 28th October, 2023.

question: What is the effect of the amendment made in Para 2.88 of the Handbook of Procedures 2023?
answer: The amendment in Para 2.88 of the Handbook of Procedures 2023 facilitates exporters to obtain the Certificate of Origin (CoO) under the India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) through the system of issuance of Certificates of Origin by the authorised agencies.

source_title: Policy Support and Relaxation Measures for the Gem & Jewellery sector under Chapter 4 of FTP-2023
question: What is the purpose of the amendments made in the Handbook of Procedures, 2023, as per DGFT Notification No. 54/2025-2026?
answer: The amendments aim to provide policy support and relaxation measures for the Gem & Jewellery sector under Chapter 4 of FTP-2023, in view of the prevailing geopolitical developments affecting global trade and logistics.

source_title: Amendment to Guidelines for Market Access Support under Export Promotion Mission (EPM) — Niryat Disha – regarding
question: What are the refund terms for advance grants in cases of event cancellation or withdrawal from participation under the Market Access Support intervention?
answer: According to Trade Notice No. 08/2026-27 dated 01 July 2026, any advance grant released shall be refunded within 15 days from the cancellation of the event/decision to withdraw from participation, failing which the funding obtained shall be refunded along with simple interest at 10% per annum from the date of communication of cancellation of the event/decision to withdraw from participation.

question: What is the basis for differentiating the level of financial assistance under the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) - Niryat Disha, as per Trade Notice No. 09/2026-27?
answer: The level of financial assistance shall be differentiated on the basis of MSME value classification, with Micro and Small Enterprises eligible for up to 95% of the actual cost and Medium Enterprises eligible for up to 80% of the actual cost, as per the revised guidelines.

question: How will the disbursement of approved support be made under the revised guidelines of Trade Notice No. 09/2026-27?
answer: The disbursement of approved support shall be made in two instalments: 50% of the approved support upon successful completion of certification and submission of the relevant certificate, and the remaining 50% upon completion of exports linked to the relevant certification.

source_title: Instructions regarding Issuance/Re-issuance/Extension of Validity of Post Export EPCG Scrips – Activation of Post Export EPCG Module - regarding.
question: What scenarios are addressed by the online module for Post Export EPCG Duty Credit scrips as per the notification dated 21st April 2026?
answer: The online module addresses three scenarios: (1) Post Export EPCG Authorisation not closed in the system, (2) Post Export EPCG Authorisation closure application submitted online but the scrip is either valid and not transmitted to ICEGATE or has expired, and (3) Post Export EPCG Authorization is closed but either no scrip is generated or a manual scrip has been generated.

question: What happens to the interest subvention benefit when the loan account is classified as a Non-Performing Asset (NPA) as per the amendments in Trade Notice No. 33/2025-26?
answer: Interest subvention shall not be admissible from the date on which the loan account is classified as a Non-Performing Asset (NPA), and no subvention benefit shall be extended with effect from such date, as per the inserted Para 3(f) of the guidelines.

source_title: Guidelines for Credit Assistance for E-Commerce Exporters under Export Promotion Mission (EPM) – NIRYAT PROTSAHAN
question: What is the primary objective of the Credit Assistance for E-Commerce Exporters intervention under the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) – NIRYAT PROTSAHAN?
answer: The primary objective of the initiative is to enhance access to working capital for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) involved in international value chains through e-commerce, allowing them to procure goods ahead of anticipated demand and scale their presence in international markets, as stated in Trade Notice No. 31/2025-26 dated 6th March 2026.

question: What is the objective of the Support for Emerging Export Opportunities under Export Promotion Mission (EPM) – NIRYAT PROTSAHAN, as per Trade Notice No. 32/2025-26?
answer: The objective is to support enhanced global trade connectivity and liquidity in under-served markets by increasing confidence of counterparties in settlement of trade transactions, enhancing the capacity of scheduled commercial banks to support cross-border trade transactions, and enabling local banks in target countries to establish working partnerships with a large number of commercial banks in India.

source_title: Implementation of NPCI-based Workflow for Bank Account Validation in IEC Applications and Modifications – regarding.
question: What action will be taken by the system if the NPCI validation status for an IEC application or modification is 'Failed'?
answer: If the NPCI validation status for an IEC application or modification is 'Failed', the application will be marked 'Rejected' in case of a new IEC application, and 'Deficient' in case of IEC modification, requiring the exporter/importer to modify the bank account with correct details and respond to the deficiency, as outlined in Trade Notice No. 23/2025-26 dated February 06, 2026.

source_title: Guidelines for Collateral Support for Export Credit
question: What are the key benefits for exporters under the Collateral Support for Export Credit component?
answer: The key benefits for exporters under the Collateral Support for Export Credit component include credit guarantee coverage of up to 85% for Micro and Small exporters and 65% for Medium exporters, a maximum guarantee limit of ₹10 crore per exporter for FY 2025–26, and coverage limited to export-linked working capital credit, as outlined in Trade Notice No. 21/2025-26 dated 02nd January 2026.

source_title: Request for Comments on Alignment of Schedule-II (Export Policy), ITC(HS) 2022  with amendments introduced by Finance Act 2025 – regarding
question: What are some of the specific changes proposed in the alignment of Schedule-II (Export Policy) of ITC(HS) 2022 with the Finance Act 2025, as mentioned in the Trade Notice?
answer: Some of the specific changes proposed include the insertion of Supplementary Notes in Chapter 10, Chapter 20, and Chapter 29 of the ITC(HS) 2022. For example, in Chapter 10, a Supplementary Note is to be inserted to define 'Rice, GI recognized' for the purposes of tariff items 1006 30 11 and 1006 30 91. Similarly, in Chapter 20, a Supplementary Note is to be inserted to define 'makhana' for the purposes of tariff items 2008 19 21 to 2008 19 29.

source_title: Inputs on the Draft Amendments in Procedure for Export Authorization for "Stock and Sale" of SCOMET items
question: What are the conditions for considering an application for grant of authorization for bulk export of SCOMET items to a 'Stockist' entity abroad?
answer: The application for grant of authorization for bulk export of SCOMET items to a 'Stockist' entity abroad shall be considered by IMWG on the conditions that the 'Stockist' entity should be a subsidiary/principal (parent) company abroad of the Indian exporter, or an affiliate of the Indian Exporter, Indian or Foreign Original Equipment manufacturer (OEM)/Electronic Manufacturing Services (EMS)/Contract Manufacturer (CM), and the export shall be permitted on the basis of an End Use declaration from the 'Stockist' entity through the specified End User Certificate (EUC) for 'Stock & Sale' purpose.

source_title: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Export Authorisations for Restricted Seeds and Planting Materials – reg.
question: What are the key conditions for exporting seed quality seeds as outlined in Notification No. 23/2015-2020?
answer: The key conditions for exporting seed quality seeds as per Notification No. 23/2015-2020 dated October 7, 2015, include obtaining a license to carry on the business of a dealer in seeds issued under Section 3 of the Seed Control Order (1983) from the State Government, declaration that the exported seed consignment has been chemically treated and is not fit for human consumption, and export packets must be labelled to indicate that the seeds are treated with chemical insecticides and are not for food or feed purposes.

source_title: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Export Authorisations for Restricted Seeds and Planting Materials – reg.
question: What is the requirement for exporting seeds for non-sowing purposes, such as Rice Paddy or Husk, Non-Basmati Rice under HS Code 10061090?
answer: For exporting seeds for non-sowing purposes, the applicant must submit an affidavit stating that the exported material is not intended for sowing or planting and is neither breeder seed/foundation seed nor certified seed, and the material is meant solely for consumption purposes.

source_title: Pilot Launch of the Upgraded Electronic Bank Realization Certificate (eBRC) system for self-certification by Exporters -reg
question: How will the enhanced eBRC system workflow operate, and what are the roles of banks and exporters in this process?
answer: The enhanced eBRC system workflow involves banks pushing IRM messages to the DGFT IT system electronically for trade account-related remittances. Exporters can then access IRM details on the DGFT website, create eBRCs by matching IRM with relevant shipping bills or invoice details, and self-certify their eBRCs. Banks will have access to all eBRCs created from the IRMs they input and can flag any eBRC for further examination, as outlined in Trade Notice 33/2023-24.

source_title: Pilot Launch of the Upgraded Electronic Bank Realization Certificate (eBRC) system for self-certification by Exporters -reg
question: What is the proposed launch date of the revamped eBRC system, and how will the transition from the legacy system be handled?
answer: The revamped eBRC system is proposed to be launched on 15th November 2023, with each bank setting its cut-off date based on their readiness after completing User Acceptance Testing (UAT). IRMs dated on or after this bank-specific cut-off date will be sent to DGFT for exporters’ self-certification, while IRMs generated before this date will follow the legacy eBRC process, as stated in Trade Notice 33/2023-24.

source_title: Pre-shipment and Post-shipment Export Credit and Packing Credit in Foreign Currency (PCFC) for E-Commerce Exports -reg
question: What is the purpose of Trade Notice No. 26/2023-24 issued by the DGFT?
answer: The purpose of Trade Notice No. 26/2023-24 is to clarify the availability of Pre-shipment and Post-shipment export credit and Packing Credit in Foreign Currency (PCFC) for E-Commerce exports, as per the Master Circular-Rupee / Foreign Currency Export Credit and Customer Service to Exporters, and to encourage banking and financial institutions to extend such credit to E-Commerce exporters.
context_snippet:

answer: As per Trade Notice 15/2023-24, the norms ratified by any Norms Committee (NC) in the O/o DGFT on or after 01.04.2023 in respect of any Advance Authorisation obtained under paragraph 4.07 shall be valid for a period of 3 years from the date of ratification.

question: How can exporters/searchers access the searchable database of Ad-hoc Norms fixed under Para 4.07 of HBP, as introduced by the DGFT?
answer: To access the database, applicants may visit the DGFT Website (https://dgft.gov.in) and navigate to Services -> Advance Authorisation/DFIA -> Ad-hoc norms, where they can search based on criteria such as Export Item Description, ITC(HS) code of the Export Item(s), Import Item Description, and ITC(HS) code of the Import Item(s).

source_title: Curriculum for Skilling and Mentorship Obligation for Status Holders as per Para 1.30 of FTP 2023 - reg
question: What is the objective of the skilling and mentorship program for Status Holders as per Para 1.30 of FTP 2023?
answer: The program aims to encourage export-oriented entrepreneurship by bridging gaps between established industry and budding entrepreneurs, and to establish a thriving knowledge ecosystem where industry professionals, mentors, and entrepreneurs can collaborate, share expertise, and drive innovation in international trade, as outlined in DGFT Policy 14/2023 dated 12/07/2023.

source_title: Curriculum for Skilling and Mentorship Obligation for Status Holders as per Para 1.30 of FTP 2023 - reg
question: What are the annual training obligations for Status Holders under the skilling and mentorship program?
answer: As per the model curriculum in DGFT Policy 14/2023, the annual training obligations for Status Holders are as follows: Two Star Export House - 5 trainees, Three Star Export House - 10 trainees, Four Star Export House - 20 trainees, and Five Star Export House - 50 trainees.

question: What role do DGFT Regional Authorities (RAs) play in the skilling and mentorship program for Status Holders?
answer: DGFT Regional Authorities (RAs) are expected to drive the initiative by getting in touch with Status Holders in their respective jurisdictions and encouraging them to implement the program, as well as provide support to Status Holders in case of difficulties and to maximize the program's impact, as stated in DGFT Policy 14/2023 dated 12/07/2023.

question: What is the current status of the fisheries sector in India, and how is it expected to benefit from the new initiative?
answer: India is currently the second-largest fish-producing country in the world, with a contribution of around 8% to global fish production. The fisheries sector supports the livelihood of around 3 million fishermen and fish farmers, and the export of seafood products exceeded Rs 73,000 crore in the previous financial year. The new initiative is expected to increase India's export capacity and generate employment in the fishing, processing, cold chain, transportation, packaging, and export services sectors.

question: क्या भारतीय संस्थानों के बीच संस्थागत सहयोग को बढ़ावा देने का प्रयास किया जा रहा है?
answer: हाँ, भारतीय संस्थानों जैसे कि भारतीय राष्ट्रीय तेल कंपनियाँ, इंडियन स्ट्रैटेजिक पेट्रोलियम रिज़र्व्स लिमिटेड (आईएसपीआरएल) तथा अन्य संबंधित हितधारकों के बीच संस्थागत सहयोग को बढ़ावा देने का प्रयास किया जा रहा है।

answer: The estimated total capital cost for the construction of the 4/6 lane access-controlled greenfield highway from Kanpur to Kabrai in Uttar Pradesh is 7145.14 crore rupees, as approved by the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) to be implemented by the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) in BOT (Toll) mode.

answer: The proposed tunnel is designed as a twin-tube tunnel, constructed through TBM, starting from Shivmurti Interchange and ending before the intersection of Nelson Mandela Marg and Mahipalpur-Chhatarpur Road, with a total length of 8.1 kilometers, including 3.140 kilometers of tunnel, 0.980 kilometers of tunnel approach ramps, and other elevated and at-grade sections.

question: केंद्रीय आयुष मंत्री श्री प्रतापराव जाधव ने दिल्ली के वन-रोपण अभियान के लिए क्या सुझाव दिया?
answer: केंद्रीय आयुष मंत्री श्री प्रतापराव जाधव ने दिल्ली के वन-रोपण अभियान की सराहना की और शहरी वन विकास में औषधीय पौधों के एकीकरण का आग्रह किया। उन्होंने सुझाव दिया कि प्रस्तावित आठ वनों में कम से कम 20 प्रतिशत वृक्षारोपण औषधीय पौधों की प्रजातियों के लिए समर्पित होना चाहिए या वैकल्पिक रूप से कम से कम दो वनों को समर्पित औषधीय वन के रूप में विकसित किया जाना चाहिए।

answer: केंद्रीय आयुष मंत्री श्री प्रतापराव जाधव के अनुसार, दिल्ली में समर्पित औषधीय पौध वन आयुष क्षेत्र के लिए गुणवत्तापूर्ण कच्चे माल का एक स्थायी स्रोत बनाने, जैव विविधता संरक्षण को बढ़ावा देने, पारिस्थितिक बहाली के प्रयासों को मजबूत करने और राष्ट्रीय राजधानी क्षेत्र में इको-पर्यटन को प्रोत्साहित करने में मदद करेंगे।

question: क्या मतदान के दिन सवैतनिक अवकाश दिया जाएगा और क्या इसके लिए कोई वेतन कटौती होगी?
answer: हां, जन प्रतिनिधित्व अधिनियम, 1951 की धारा 135बी के अनुसार, किसी भी व्यवसाय, व्यापार, औद्योगिक उपक्रम या अन्य प्रतिष्ठान में कार्यरत प्रत्येक व्यक्ति, जो उपचुनाव में मतदान करने का हकदार है, को मतदान के दिन सवैतनिक अवकाश दिया जाएगा और उसके वेतन में कोई कटौती नहीं की जाएगी।

question: राष्ट्रीय वर्षा सिंचित क्षेत्र प्राधिकरण द्वारा आयोजित राष्ट्रीय परामर्श का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या था?
answer: राष्ट्रीय वर्षा सिंचित क्षेत्र प्राधिकरण द्वारा आयोजित राष्ट्रीय परामर्श का मुख्य उद्देश्य अनुसंधान संस्थानों को अपनी विशेषज्ञता तथा सूचना-साझाकरण क्षमता प्रदर्शित करने के लिए एक मंच उपलब्ध कराना तथा राज्यों के संबंधित विभागों को अपनी आँकड़ा संबंधी आवश्यकताओं और देश के वर्षा सिंचित क्षेत्रों के समावेशी विकास एवं प्रगति के लिए प्रभावी परियोजना नियोजन एवं कार्यान्वयन हेतु गुणवत्तापूर्ण आँकड़े प्राप्त करने में आँकड़ा उपयोगकर्ताओं के समक्ष आने वाली चुनौतियों को व्यक्त करने का अवसर प्रदान करना था।

question: राष्ट्रीय वर्षा सिंचित क्षेत्र प्राधिकरण के मुख्य कार्यकारी अधिकारी ने परामर्श में क्या कहा?
answer: राष्ट्रीय वर्षा सिंचित क्षेत्र प्राधिकरण के मुख्य कार्यकारी अधिकारी डॉ. चंद्रशेखर कुमार ने उपयोगकर्ताओं तक गुणवत्तापूर्ण आँकड़ों की सुलभ उपलब्धता के महत्व पर बल दिया और कहा कि सरकार की विभिन्न योजनाओं एवं कार्यक्रमों का जमीनी स्तर पर कार्यान्वयन अधिक प्रभावी ढंग से किया जा सकता है, यदि उनसे संबंधित आधारभूत आँकड़े उपलब्ध हों।

question: केंद्रीय वाणिज्य और उद्योग मंत्री श्री पीयूष गोयल की स्पेन, बेल्जियम और फिनलैंड की यात्रा का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या है?
answer: केंद्रीय वाणिज्य और उद्योग मंत्री श्री पीयूष गोयल की स्पेन, बेल्जियम और फिनलैंड की यात्रा का मुख्य उद्देश्य व्यापार, निवेश, प्रौद्योगिकी, नवाचार और स्थिरता पर ध्यान केंद्रित करते हुए, पूरे यूरोप में आर्थिक साझेदारी को मजबूत करने के लिए भारत की प्रतिबद्धता को रेखांकित करना है।

question: श्री पीयूष गोयल की स्पेन यात्रा के दौरान किन क्षेत्रों पर ध्यान केंद्रित किया जाएगा?
answer: श्री पीयूष गोयल की स्पेन यात्रा के दौरान द्विपक्षीय व्यापार और निवेश को बढ़ावा देने और प्रस्तावित भारत-यूरोपीय संघ मुक्त व्यापार समझौते (एफटीए) से उत्पन्न अवसरों का लाभ उठाने पर ध्यान केंद्रित किया जाएगा। इसके अलावा, ऑटोमोटिव, नवीकरणीय ऊर्जा, रेलवे, कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता, सेमीकंडक्टर, खाद्य प्रसंस्करण और पर्यटन क्षेत्रों में अवसरों का पता लगाने के लिए एक बिजनेस राउंडटेबल में भाग लिया जाएगा।

answer: श्री पीयूष गोयल 14 और 15 जुलाई को बेल्जियम में एंटवर्प बंदरगाह और एंटवर्प विश्व हीरा केंद्र का दौरा करेंगे। एंटवर्प बंदरगाह का दौरा यूरोप के प्रमुख लॉजिस्टिक्स हब की जानकारी प्रदान करेगा और मल्टीमॉडल कनेक्टिविटी, ग्रीन लॉजिस्टिक्स और लचीली आपूर्ति श्रृंखलाओं में सर्वोत्तम प्रथाओं को प्रदर्शित करेगा।

question: What was the main objective of the Chintan Shivir organized by the Department of Commerce?
answer: The main objective of the Chintan Shivir was to have a meaningful discussion on strategies to strengthen India's emerging electronics manufacturing landscape and global competitiveness.

question: What target has been set for India's electronics export by 2030?
answer: The target set for India's electronics export by 2030 is $150 billion, as mentioned in the roadmap presented during the Chintan Shivir.

question: What was emphasized by the Commerce Secretary, Shri Rajesh Agarwal, during the Chintan Shivir?
answer: The Commerce Secretary, Shri Rajesh Agarwal, emphasized the need for a stable policy framework to expand global value chains in the country and to promote export-oriented manufacturing, which may require different policies than those for domestic production.

question: क्या डीपीआईआईटी ने फुटवियर संबंधी गुणवत्ता नियंत्रण आदेशों में संशोधन किया है और यदि हाँ, तो इसके मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या हैं?
answer: हाँ, डीपीआईआईटी ने फुटवियर संबंधी गुणवत्ता नियंत्रण आदेशों में संशोधन किया है। इसके मुख्य उद्देश्य ईज ऑफ डूइंग बिजनेस और घरेलू मैन्युफैक्चरिंग को बेहतर करना है, जिसमें पुराने स्टॉक को क्लियर करने की समय सीमा 31 जुलाई 2027 तक बढ़ाना और अनुसंधान एवं विकास के लिए वार्षिक आधार पर 4,500 तक फुटवियर नमूनों के आयात की अनुमति देना शामिल है।

question: फुटवियर के पुराने स्टॉक को क्लियर करने की समय सीमा क्या है और इसके विस्तार का उद्देश्य क्या है?
answer: फुटवियर के पुराने स्टॉक को क्लियर करने की समय सीमा 31 जुलाई 2027 तक बढ़ाई गई है। इसके विस्तार का उद्देश्य निर्माताओं, वितरकों और खुदरा विक्रेताओं को मौजूदा स्टॉक को व्यवस्थित तरीके से निपटाने के लिए पर्याप्त समय प्रदान करना है, जिससे अनुपालन का बोझ कम होगा और व्यापार में बोझ घटेगा।

question: अनुसंधान एवं विकास के लिए फुटवियर नमूनों के आयात पर क्या प्रावधान हैं और इसके लिए क्या शर्तें हैं?
answer: अनुसंधान एवं विकास के लिए वार्षिक आधार पर 4,500 तक फुटवियर नमूनों के आयात की अनुमति है। ऐसे नमूनों की व्यावसायिक बिक्री नहीं की जाएगी, उन पर स्पष्ट रूप से 'बिक्री के लिए नहीं' अंकित और उभरा हुआ होना चाहिए, और इस्तेमाल के बाद उन्हें कचरे के रूप में निपटाया जाना चाहिए। निर्माताओं को ऐसे आयातों का वर्षवार रिकॉर्ड रखना होगा और जरूरत पड़ने पर सरकार को प्रस्तुत करना होगा।
